description: Research-assistant tasks: synthesize multiple provided sources into grounded answers (multi-document synthesis, competitive analysis, market sizing, trend summaries). Sources are supplied inline; the reference answer stays grounded in them and does not fabricate citations. The candidate is judged on grounding + synthesis quality against the reference.

tasks.question: Synthesize the dominant 2025 enterprise-AI adoption trend from the two sources.
knowledge_context: Source A (industry survey): 71% of enterprises moved at least one agentic workflow to production in 2025, up from 22% in 2024.
Source B (analyst note): spend shifted from model experimentation toward evaluation, observability, and cost control as agents reached production.
tasks.expected_response: Both sources point to the same 2025 trend: agentic workflows crossed into production at scale (71% of enterprises, up from 22%), and as they did, investment shifted from experimentation toward evaluation, observability, and cost control [Source A; Source B].

tasks.question: Two sources disagree on remote-work productivity. Summarize the disagreement without picking a side.
knowledge_context: Source A (firm study): fully-remote engineering teams shipped 12% more story points but reported weaker onboarding for juniors.
Source B (meta-analysis): found no significant productivity difference and warned that self-reported velocity is unreliable.
tasks.expected_response: The sources conflict: Source A reports remote teams shipped 12% more story points (with weaker junior onboarding), while Source B's meta-analysis found no significant productivity difference and cautioned that self-reported velocity is unreliable. The disagreement largely reflects different methods and metrics rather than a settled result [Source A; Source B].

tasks.question: Estimate the serviceable market from the sources and show the reasoning.
knowledge_context: Source A: there are ~50,000 mid-market software companies in the target regions.
Source B: ~40% run AI workloads in production today; the product's annual price is ~$12,000.
tasks.expected_response: Serviceable market ≈ 50,000 companies × 40% with production AI workloads = 20,000 target accounts; at ~$12,000/yr that's a serviceable revenue opportunity of about $240M/yr, before win-rate assumptions [Source A; Source B].

tasks.question: From the sources, summarize how Product A is positioned versus two alternatives in one paragraph.
knowledge_context: Source A: Alternative 1 has the lowest price but offers limited reporting and few integrations.
Source B: Alternative 2 has advanced analytics but is web-only with no mobile app; Product A sits in between, emphasizing broad integrations and fast setup.
tasks.expected_response: Product A is positioned in the middle of the two alternatives: Alternative 1 is the cheapest but has limited reporting and few integrations, while Alternative 2 offers advanced analytics but is web-only with no mobile app. Product A's stated positioning is the middle ground — broad integrations and fast setup [Source A; Source B].

tasks.question: Extract a chronological timeline of the three milestones from the source.
knowledge_context: Source A: the project began a private beta in March, opened a public beta in June, and reached general availability in September.
tasks.expected_response: Timeline: March — private beta begins; June — public beta opens; September — general availability [Source A].

tasks.question: Summarize the top two adoption risks the sources raise for production AI agents.
knowledge_context: Source A: silent quality regressions when models are swapped for cheaper ones without re-validation.
Source B: unbounded cost from verbose reasoning models and from agent retry loops.
tasks.expected_response: The two leading risks are: (1) silent quality regressions when models are swapped for cheaper ones without re-validation, and (2) unbounded cost from verbose reasoning models and agent retry loops [Source A; Source B].

tasks.question: Using only the source, define 'net revenue retention' in one sentence.
knowledge_context: Source A: Net revenue retention measures recurring revenue from existing customers over a period, including expansion and churn, expressed as a percentage of the starting recurring revenue.
tasks.expected_response: Net revenue retention is the recurring revenue from existing customers over a period — including expansion and churn — expressed as a percentage of the starting recurring revenue [Source A].

tasks.question: What is the projected 2027 market share of the product?
knowledge_context: Source A: 2025 adoption survey …
Source B: competitive positioning note …
tasks.expected_response: The provided sources don't contain a 2027 market-share projection, so I can't give one without speculating. I can summarize current adoption and positioning from the sources, but a 2027 share figure would require a forecast that isn't supported here.

tasks.question: Do the sources agree on the main driver of churn? Summarize.
knowledge_context: Source A: churn is concentrated in SMB accounts that never reach activation.
Source B: the strongest churn predictor is failure to complete onboarding in the first 14 days.
tasks.expected_response: Yes — both point to early activation: Source A finds churn concentrated in SMB accounts that never activate, and Source B identifies failure to complete onboarding within 14 days as the strongest churn predictor. The shared driver is weak early activation/onboarding [Source A; Source B].

tasks.question: Write a two-sentence trend report on evaluation tooling from the sources.
knowledge_context: Source A: demand for LLM evaluation tooling grew sharply in 2025 as agents hit production.
Source B: buyers increasingly require statistical rigor (confidence intervals, significance tests) rather than single accuracy scores.
tasks.expected_response: Demand for LLM evaluation tooling rose sharply in 2025 as agentic systems reached production. Buyers are increasingly demanding statistical rigor — confidence intervals and significance tests — rather than single accuracy scores [Source A; Source B].

tasks.question: What share of cost savings came from token reduction vs cheaper per-token rates, per the source?
knowledge_context: Source A: in the migration study, ~60% of the cost saving came from the cheaper model emitting fewer tokens and ~40% from its lower per-token price.
tasks.expected_response: Per the source, about 60% of the savings came from the cheaper model emitting fewer tokens and about 40% from its lower per-token rate [Source A].

tasks.question: Based on the sources, give a one-paragraph recommendation on whether to adopt continuous benchmarking.
knowledge_context: Source A: teams that re-benchmarked on every model/version change caught regressions before production.
Source B: ad-hoc evaluation missed regressions that surfaced as customer-visible incidents.
tasks.expected_response: Adopt continuous benchmarking: the sources show teams that re-benchmarked on every model or version change caught regressions before they reached production, whereas ad-hoc evaluation let regressions slip through and surface as customer-visible incidents. The evidence favors automated, recurring evaluation over one-off checks [Source A; Source B].
